ogTitle: Pooncarie vs Port Hacking: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pooncarie and Port Hacking.
heading: Pooncarie vs Port Hacking — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pooncarie, New South Wales and Port Hacking, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pooncarie has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Port Hacking (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pooncarie or Port Hacking safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pooncarie has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Port Hacking scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pooncarie scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Port Hacking scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pooncarie or Port Hacking?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pooncarie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
